description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses variable Docker images $CI_REGISTRY/$BUILD_IMAGES_PROJECT with tags $ANDROID_BUILD, $CENTOS7_BUILD, $CENTOS8_BUILD, $CENTOS9_BUILD, $FEDORA_BUILD, $MINGW32_BUILD and $MINGW64_BUILD, all running on saas-linux-small-amd64 runners. In the test-android stage define four jobs—Android/arm, Android/arm64, Android/x86, Android/x86_64—each allowing failure, excluding tags and schedules, always publishing artifacts from android/*-linux-android*/out/*, and executing `make -C android ARCH=<arch> TOOLCHAIN=$ANDROID_TOOLCHAINDIR`. In the test-rest stage define the CentOS7/GnuTLS, CentOS7/OpenSSL, CentOS8/GnuTLS, CentOS8/OpenSSL, CentOS9/GnuTLS, CentOS9/OpenSSL, Fedora/GnuTLS, Fedora/GnuTLS/clang, Fedora/GnuTLS/ibmtss, Fedora/OpenSSL and Fedora/OpenSSL/clang jobs, each using its distro‑specific image, collecting logs on failure, and running a sequence of `./autogen.sh`, a `./configure` with the appropriate flags (e.g., --with-java, --without-gnutls, --with-openssl, CC=clang, etc.), `make -j4`, `sysctl net.ipv6.conf.all.disable_ipv6=0`, `mv /etc/ppp /etc/ppp.DISABLED`, and `make VERBOSE=1 XFAIL_TESTS="…" -j4 check`. Add a Coverity job in the coverity stage that runs only on schedules or when the coverity keyword is present, downloads the Coverity analysis tool, builds the project with `cov-build`, archives the cov-int directory, and uploads it via curl, storing cov-int/*.txt on failure. Finally, add MinGW32/GnuTLS, MinGW32/OpenSSL and MinGW64/GnuTLS jobs in the test-windows stage that always keep logs and installer executables, mount binfmt_misc, register wine, configure with mingw32-configure (or remove wine for MinGW64), set WINEPATH, build with `make -j4`, and run `make VERBOSE=1 XFAIL_TESTS="list-taps.exe wintun-names.exe" -j4 check`.
